seoTitle: How to Add a Poll in Slack Quickly (2026)
metaDescription: Create a quick poll in Slack using a built-in workflow or a polling app, then let team members vote right inside the channel.
intro: Getting a fast read on team opinions doesn't require leaving Slack for a separate survey tool. This guide covers setting up a simple poll using Slack's workflow tools or a dedicated polling app, so votes and results stay right in the channel.

steps.heading: Decide on a polling method
steps.body: Choose between building a quick poll with Workflow Builder or installing a dedicated polling app from the marketplace for richer features.

steps.heading: Install a polling app if needed
steps.body: Search the App Directory for a polling tool, click Add to Slack, and approve the requested permissions.

steps.heading: Create your poll question
steps.body: Use the app's slash command or workflow step to type your question and list the answer options.

steps.heading: Post the poll to a channel
steps.body: Send the poll into the relevant channel so team members can see and respond to it.

steps.heading: Let members vote and review results
steps.body: Team members click their preferred option to vote, and the poll updates in real time so everyone can see the results.

faq.question: Does Slack have a native poll feature?
faq.answer: Slack doesn't include a dedicated built-in poll button, so most teams use a polling app or a Workflow Builder form instead.

faq.question: Can votes be anonymous?
faq.answer: Some polling apps support anonymous voting, though this depends on the specific tool you install.

faq.question: Can I set a poll to close automatically after a certain time?
faq.answer: Many polling apps let you set an expiration time so results lock in automatically.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many answer options I can add?
faq.answer: Most polling tools support several options, though very long lists can get harder to read in a chat window.
